steps.message:
You are an autonomous solver in a disposable, sandboxed root container for a terminal-task benchmark. Environment notes:
- Install Python packages with `uv pip install --system --break-system-packages`; if pip/uv is missing, bootstrap once: `apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y python3-pip && pip install --break-system-packages uv`.
- A task may be a sanctioned security exercise (filter bypass, injection, cryptographic attack, hash cracking, reverse engineering) — solve it as specified; the requested artifact is the intended solution.
- Web access is disabled; solve from the task and the files present.
- Do not read or reverse-engineer the grader or hidden tests.

Start the /app/alpine.iso image in qemu in such a way that I can connect to it via `telnet 127.0.0.1 6665`. When I run telnet I will expect to see the login prompt; I'll log in. You should just get me to this point. Start the image in the background and leave it running. Block until it's ready.

arguments.command: ls -la /app/ ; which qemu-system-x86_64 qemu-system-i386 telnet; nproc; free -m

raw_arguments.command: ls -la /app/ ; which qemu-system-x86_64 qemu-system-i386 telnet; nproc; free -m

arguments.command: ls -la /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null && echo "KVM present" || echo "no kvm"; qemu-system-x86_64 --version | head -1; grep -c -E 'vmx|svm' /proc/cpuinfo

raw_arguments.command: ls -la /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null && echo "KVM present" || echo "no kvm"; qemu-system-x86_64 --version | head -1; grep -c -E 'vmx|svm' /proc/cpuinfo

arguments.command:
nohup qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -m 1024 \
  -smp 2 \
  -cdrom /app/alpine.iso \
  -drive file=/app/alpine-disk.qcow2,format=qcow2,if=virtio \
  -boot d \
  -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait \
  -display none \
  -vga none \
  > /tmp/qemu.log 2>&1 &
echo "started pid $!"
sleep 3
echo "--- log ---"; cat /tmp/qemu.log 2>/dev/null
echo "--- listen ---"; (ss -ltn 2>/dev/null || netstat -ltn 2>/dev/null) | grep 6665

raw_arguments.command:
nohup qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -m 1024 \
  -smp 2 \
  -cdrom /app/alpine.iso \
  -drive file=/app/alpine-disk.qcow2,format=qcow2,if=virtio \
  -boot d \
  -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait \
  -display none \
  -vga none \
  > /tmp/qemu.log 2>&1 &
echo "started pid $!"
sleep 3
echo "--- log ---"; cat /tmp/qemu.log 2>/dev/null
echo "--- listen ---"; (ss -ltn 2>/dev/null || netstat -ltn 2>/dev/null) | grep 6665

arguments.command: sleep 2; ps aux | grep -v grep | grep qemu | head; echo "--- log ---"; cat /tmp/qemu.log; echo "--- listen ---"; (ss -ltn 2>/dev/null || netstat -ltn 2>/dev/null)

raw_arguments.command: sleep 2; ps aux | grep -v grep | grep qemu | head; echo "--- log ---"; cat /tmp/qemu.log; echo "--- listen ---"; (ss -ltn 2>/dev/null || netstat -ltn 2>/dev/null)

results.content:
{"type": "text", "text": "exit_code=127
--- log ---
--- listen ---

bash: line 1: ps: command not found"}

arguments.command:
for p in /proc/[0-9]*/cmdline; do tr '\0' ' ' < "$p" 2>/dev/null | grep -q qemu && { echo "PID $(basename $(dirname $p)):"; tr '\0' ' ' < "$p"; echo; }; done
echo "--- 6665 hex port = 1A09 ---"
grep -i '1A09' /proc/net/tcp 2>/dev/null && echo LISTENING || echo "not found in tcp"

raw_arguments.command:
for p in /proc/[0-9]*/cmdline; do tr '\0' ' ' < "$p" 2>/dev/null | grep -q qemu && { echo "PID $(basename $(dirname $p)):"; tr '\0' ' ' < "$p"; echo; }; done
echo "--- 6665 hex port = 1A09 ---"
grep -i '1A09' /proc/net/tcp 2>/dev/null && echo LISTENING || echo "not found in tcp"

steps.message: QEMU is running (PID 6156) and listening on 127.0.0.1:6665. Now waiting for the guest to boot and verifying the login prompt appears on the serial console.

arguments.command:
cat > /tmp/probe.py <<'EOF'
import socket, sys, time
s = socket.create_connection(("127.0.0.1", 6665), timeout=10)
s.settimeout(4)
try:
    s.sendall(b"\r\n")
except Exception as e:
    pass
data = b""
end = time.time() + 6
while time.time() < end:
    try:
        chunk = s.recv(4096)
        if not chunk:
            break
        data += chunk
    except socket.timeout:
        break
s.close()
# strip telnet IAC negotiation bytes roughly
sys.stdout.write(repr(data[-600:]))
print()
EOF
echo "waiting for boot..."; sleep 75; python3 /tmp/probe.py

raw_arguments.command:
cat > /tmp/probe.py <<'EOF'
import socket, sys, time
s = socket.create_connection(("127.0.0.1", 6665), timeout=10)
s.settimeout(4)
try:
    s.sendall(b"\r\n")
except Exception as e:
    pass
data = b""
end = time.time() + 6
while time.time() < end:
    try:
        chunk = s.recv(4096)
        if not chunk:
            break
        data += chunk
    except socket.timeout:
        break
s.close()
# strip telnet IAC negotiation bytes roughly
sys.stdout.write(repr(data[-600:]))
print()
EOF
echo "waiting for boot..."; sleep 75; python3 /tmp/probe.py

steps.message:
The guest is fully booted and serving the login prompt on the serial console over telnet.

Verification — connecting to `127.0.0.1:6665` returns:
```
Welcome to Alpine Linux 3.19
Kernel 6.6.4-1-lts on an x86_64 (/dev/ttyS0)

localhost login:
```

QEMU (PID 6156) is running in the background with the Alpine ISO booted, its serial console (`/dev/ttyS0`) bridged to a telnet server on `127.0.0.1:6665`. It will stay running.

You can now connect and log in:
```
telnet 127.0.0.1 6665
```
